fields.name: servicecontrol_services_check
fields.description: Checks whether an operation on a service should be allowed to proceed based on the configuration of the service and related policies. It must be called before the operation is executed. If feasible, the client should cache the check results and reuse them for 60 seconds. In case of any server errors, the client should rely on the cached results for much longer time to avoid outage. WARNING: There is general 60s delay for the configuration and policy propagation, therefore callers MUST NOT depend on the `Check` method having the latest policy information. NOTE: the CheckRequest has the size limit (wire-format byte size) of 1MB. This method requires the `servicemanagement.services.check` permission on the specified service. For more information, see [Cloud IAM](https://cloud.google.com/iam).

args.name: serviceName
args.description: The service name as specified in its service configuration. For example, `"pubsub.googleapis.com"`. See [google.api.Service](https://cloud.google.com/service-management/reference/rpc/google.api#google.api.Service) for the definition of a service name.

fields.name: servicecontrol_services_report
fields.description: Reports operation results to Google Service Control, such as logs and metrics. It should be called after an operation is completed. If feasible, the client should aggregate reporting data for up to 5 seconds to reduce API traffic. Limiting aggregation to 5 seconds is to reduce data loss during client crashes. Clients should carefully choose the aggregation time window to avoid data loss risk more than 0.01% for business and compliance reasons. NOTE: the ReportRequest has the size limit (wire-format byte size) of 1MB. This method requires the `servicemanagement.services.report` permission on the specified service. For more information, see [Google Cloud IAM](https://cloud.google.com/iam).

args.name: serviceName
args.description: The service name as specified in its service configuration. For example, `"pubsub.googleapis.com"`. See [google.api.Service](https://cloud.google.com/service-management/reference/rpc/google.api#google.api.Service) for the definition of a service name.

fields.name: servicecontrol_services_allocateQuota
fields.description: Attempts to allocate quota for the specified consumer. It should be called before the operation is executed. This method requires the `servicemanagement.services.quota` permission on the specified service. For more information, see [Cloud IAM](https://cloud.google.com/iam). **NOTE:** The client **must** fail-open on server errors `INTERNAL`, `UNKNOWN`, `DEADLINE_EXCEEDED`, and `UNAVAILABLE`. To ensure system reliability, the server may inject these errors to prohibit any hard dependency on the quota functionality.

args.name: serviceName
args.description: Name of the service as specified in the service configuration. For example, `"pubsub.googleapis.com"`. See google.api.Service for the definition of a service name.

types.name: CheckResponse
types.description: Response message for the Check method.

fields.name: operationId
fields.description: The same operation_id value used in the CheckRequest. Used for logging and diagnostics purposes.

fields.name: checkErrors
fields.description: Indicate the decision of the check. If no check errors are present, the service should process the operation. Otherwise the service should use the list of errors to determine the appropriate action.

fields.name: serviceRolloutId
fields.description: The current service rollout id used to process the request.

fields.name: serviceConfigId
fields.description: The actual config id used to process the request.

types.name: QuotaInfo
types.description: Contains the quota information for a quota check response.

fields.name: limitExceeded
fields.description: Quota Metrics that have exceeded quota limits. For QuotaGroup-based quota, this is QuotaGroup.name For QuotaLimit-based quota, this is QuotaLimit.name See: google.api.Quota Deprecated: Use quota_metrics to get per quota group limit exceeded status.

fields.name: quotaConsumed
fields.description: Map of quota group name to the actual number of tokens consumed. If the quota check was not successful, then this will not be populated due to no quota consumption. We are not merging this field with 'quota_metrics' field because of the complexity of scaling in Chemist client code base. For simplicity, we will keep this field for Castor (that scales quota usage) and 'quota_metrics' for SuperQuota (that doesn't scale quota usage).

fields.name: quotaMetrics
fields.description: Quota metrics to indicate the usage. Depending on the check request, one or more of the following metrics will be included: 1. For rate quota, per quota group or per quota metric incremental usage will be specified using the following delta metric: "serviceruntime.googleapis.com/api/consumer/quota_used_count" 2. For allocation quota, per quota metric total usage will be specified using the following gauge metric: "serviceruntime.googleapis.com/allocation/consumer/quota_used_count" 3. For both rate quota and allocation quota, the quota limit reached condition will be specified using the following boolean metric: "serviceruntime.googleapis.com/quota/exceeded"

types.name: mutation_servicecontrol_services_check_quotaInfo_quotaExtractionState
types.description: Output only. Indicates the state of the quota extraction.

types.name: JSON
types.description: The `JSON` scalar type represents JSON values as specified by [ECMA-404](http://www.ecma-international.org/publications/files/ECMA-ST/ECMA-404.pdf).

types.name: MetricValueSet
types.description: Represents a set of metric values in the same metric. Each metric value in the set should have a unique combination of start time, end time, and label values.

fields.name: metricName
fields.description: The metric name defined in the service configuration.

fields.name: metricValues
fields.description: The values in this metric.

types.name: MetricValue
types.description: Represents a single metric value.

fields.name: endTime
fields.description: The end of the time period over which this metric value's measurement applies. If not specified, google.api.servicecontrol.v1.Operation.end_time will be used.

fields.name: boolValue
fields.description: A boolean value.

fields.name: labels
fields.description: The labels describing the metric value. See comments on google.api.servicecontrol.v1.Operation.labels for the overriding relationship. Note that this map must not contain monitored resource labels.

fields.name: startTime
fields.description: The start of the time period over which this metric value's measurement applies. The time period has different semantics for different metric types (cumulative, delta, and gauge). See the metric definition documentation in the service configuration for details. If not specified, google.api.servicecontrol.v1.Operation.start_time will be used.

fields.name: doubleValue
fields.description: A double precision floating point value.

fields.name: stringValue
fields.description: A text string value.

fields.name: int64Value
fields.description: A signed 64-bit integer value.

types.name: Float
types.description: The `Float` scalar type represents signed double-precision fractional values as specified by [IEEE 754](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/IEEE_floating_point).

types.name: Money
types.description: Represents an amount of money with its currency type.

fields.name: currencyCode
fields.description: The three-letter currency code defined in ISO 4217.

fields.name: units
fields.description: The whole units of the amount. For example if `currencyCode` is `"USD"`, then 1 unit is one US dollar.

fields.name: nanos
fields.description: Number of nano (10^-9) units of the amount. The value must be between -999,999,999 and +999,999,999 inclusive. If `units` is positive, `nanos` must be positive or zero. If `units` is zero, `nanos` can be positive, zero, or negative. If `units` is negative, `nanos` must be negative or zero. For example $-1.75 is represented as `units`=-1 and `nanos`=-750,000,000.

types.name: Int
types.description: The `Int` scalar type represents non-fractional signed whole numeric values. Int can represent values between -(2^31) and 2^31 - 1.

types.name: BigInt
types.description: The `BigInt` scalar type represents non-fractional signed whole numeric values.

types.name: Distribution
types.description: Distribution represents a frequency distribution of double-valued sample points. It contains the size of the population of sample points plus additional optional information: * the arithmetic mean of the samples * the minimum and maximum of the samples * the sum-squared-deviation of the samples, used to compute variance * a histogram of the values of the sample points

fields.name: maximum
fields.description: The maximum of the population of values. Ignored if `count` is zero.

fields.name: sumOfSquaredDeviation
fields.description: The sum of squared deviations from the mean: Sum[i=1..count]((x_i - mean)^2) where each x_i is a sample values. If `count` is zero then this field must be zero, otherwise validation of the request fails.

fields.name: count
fields.description: The total number of samples in the distribution. Must be >= 0.

fields.name: exemplars
fields.description: Example points. Must be in increasing order of `value` field.

fields.name: bucketCounts
fields.description: The number of samples in each histogram bucket. `bucket_counts` are optional. If present, they must sum to the `count` value. The buckets are defined below in `bucket_option`. There are N buckets. `bucket_counts[0]` is the number of samples in the underflow bucket. `bucket_counts[1]` to `bucket_counts[N-1]` are the numbers of samples in each of the finite buckets. And `bucket_counts[N]` is the number of samples in the overflow bucket. See the comments of `bucket_option` below for more details. Any suffix of trailing zeros may be omitted.

fields.name: mean
fields.description: The arithmetic mean of the samples in the distribution. If `count` is zero then this field must be zero.

fields.name: minimum
fields.description: The minimum of the population of values. Ignored if `count` is zero.

types.name: LinearBuckets
types.description: Describing buckets with constant width.

fields.name: width
fields.description: The i'th linear bucket covers the interval [offset + (i-1) * width, offset + i * width) where i ranges from 1 to num_finite_buckets, inclusive. Must be strictly positive.

fields.name: offset
fields.description: The i'th linear bucket covers the interval [offset + (i-1) * width, offset + i * width) where i ranges from 1 to num_finite_buckets, inclusive.

fields.name: numFiniteBuckets
fields.description: The number of finite buckets. With the underflow and overflow buckets, the total number of buckets is `num_finite_buckets` + 2. See comments on `bucket_options` for details.

types.name: ExplicitBuckets
types.description: Describing buckets with arbitrary user-provided width.

fields.name: bounds
fields.description: 'bound' is a list of strictly increasing boundaries between buckets. Note that a list of length N-1 defines N buckets because of fenceposting. See comments on `bucket_options` for details. The i'th finite bucket covers the interval [bound[i-1], bound[i]) where i ranges from 1 to bound_size() - 1. Note that there are no finite buckets at all if 'bound' only contains a single element; in that special case the single bound defines the boundary between the underflow and overflow buckets. bucket number lower bound upper bound i == 0 (underflow) -inf bound[i] 0 < i < bound_size() bound[i-1] bound[i] i == bound_size() (overflow) bound[i-1] +inf

types.name: ExponentialBuckets
types.description: Describing buckets with exponentially growing width.

fields.name: scale
fields.description: The i'th exponential bucket covers the interval [scale * growth_factor^(i-1), scale * growth_factor^i) where i ranges from 1 to num_finite_buckets inclusive. Must be > 0.

fields.name: growthFactor
fields.description: The i'th exponential bucket covers the interval [scale * growth_factor^(i-1), scale * growth_factor^i) where i ranges from 1 to num_finite_buckets inclusive. Must be larger than 1.0.

fields.name: numFiniteBuckets
fields.description: The number of finite buckets. With the underflow and overflow buckets, the total number of buckets is `num_finite_buckets` + 2. See comments on `bucket_options` for details.

types.name: Exemplar
types.description: Exemplars are example points that may be used to annotate aggregated distribution values. They are metadata that gives information about a particular value added to a Distribution bucket, such as a trace ID that was active when a value was added. They may contain further information, such as a example values and timestamps, origin, etc.

fields.name: value
fields.description: Value of the exemplar point. This value determines to which bucket the exemplar belongs.

fields.name: timestamp
fields.description: The observation (sampling) time of the above value.

fields.name: attachments
fields.description: Contextual information about the example value. Examples are: Trace: type.googleapis.com/google.monitoring.v3.SpanContext Literal string: type.googleapis.com/google.protobuf.StringValue Labels dropped during aggregation: type.googleapis.com/google.monitoring.v3.DroppedLabels There may be only a single attachment of any given message type in a single exemplar, and this is enforced by the system.

types.name: CheckInfo
types.description: Contains additional information about the check operation.

fields.name: unusedArguments
fields.description: A list of fields and label keys that are ignored by the server. The client doesn't need to send them for following requests to improve performance and allow better aggregation.

fields.name: apiKeyUid
fields.description: The unique id of the api key in the format of "apikey:". This field will be populated when the consumer passed to Chemist is an API key and all the API key related validations are successful.

fields.name: ignoreApiKeyUidAsCredentialId
fields.description: Whether or not the api key should be ignored in the credential_id during reporting.

types.name: ConsumerInfo
types.description: `ConsumerInfo` provides information about the consumer.

fields.name: consumerNumber
fields.description: The consumer identity number, can be Google cloud project number, folder number or organization number e.g. 1234567890. A value of 0 indicates no consumer number is found.

fields.name: projectNumber
fields.description: The Google cloud project number, e.g. 1234567890. A value of 0 indicates no project number is found. NOTE: This field is deprecated after Chemist support flexible consumer id. New code should not depend on this field anymore.

types.name: mutation_servicecontrol_services_check_checkInfo_consumerInfo_type
types.description: The type of the consumer which should have been defined in [Google Resource Manager](https://cloud.google.com/resource-manager/).

types.name: CheckError
types.description: Defines the errors to be returned in google.api.servicecontrol.v1.CheckResponse.check_errors.

fields.name: detail
fields.description: Free-form text providing details on the error cause of the error.

fields.name: subject
fields.description: Subject to whom this error applies. See the specific code enum for more details on this field. For example: - "project:" - "folder:" - "organization:"

types.name: mutation_servicecontrol_services_check_checkErrors_items_code
types.description: The error code.

types.name: Status
types.description: The `Status` type defines a logical error model that is suitable for different programming environments, including REST APIs and RPC APIs. It is used by [gRPC](https://github.com/grpc). Each `Status` message contains three pieces of data: error code, error message, and error details. You can find out more about this error model and how to work with it in the [API Design Guide](https://cloud.google.com/apis/design/errors).

fields.name: code
fields.description: The status code, which should be an enum value of google.rpc.Code.

fields.name: message
fields.description: A developer-facing error message, which should be in English. Any user-facing error message should be localized and sent in the google.rpc.Status.details field, or localized by the client.

fields.name: details
fields.description: A list of messages that carry the error details. There is a common set of message types for APIs to use.

types.name: CheckRequest_Input
types.description: Request message for the Check method.

inputFields.name: requestProjectSettings
inputFields.description: Requests the project settings to be returned as part of the check response.

inputFields.name: serviceConfigId
inputFields.description: Specifies which version of service configuration should be used to process the request. If unspecified or no matching version can be found, the latest one will be used.

inputFields.name: skipActivationCheck
inputFields.description: Indicates if service activation check should be skipped for this request. Default behavior is to perform the check and apply relevant quota. WARNING: Setting this flag to "true" will disable quota enforcement.

types.name: Operation_Input
types.description: Represents information regarding an operation.

inputFields.name: metricValueSets
inputFields.description: Represents information about this operation. Each MetricValueSet corresponds to a metric defined in the service configuration. The data type used in the MetricValueSet must agree with the data type specified in the metric definition. Within a single operation, it is not allowed to have more than one MetricValue instances that have the same metric names and identical label value combinations. If a request has such duplicated MetricValue instances, the entire request is rejected with an invalid argument error.

inputFields.name: consumerId
inputFields.description: Identity of the consumer who is using the service. This field should be filled in for the operations initiated by a consumer, but not for service-initiated operations that are not related to a specific consumer. - This can be in one of the following formats: - project:PROJECT_ID, - project`_`number:PROJECT_NUMBER, - projects/PROJECT_ID or PROJECT_NUMBER, - folders/FOLDER_NUMBER, - organizations/ORGANIZATION_NUMBER, - api`_`key:API_KEY.

inputFields.name: labels
inputFields.description: Labels describing the operation. Only the following labels are allowed: - Labels describing monitored resources as defined in the service configuration. - Default labels of metric values. When specified, labels defined in the metric value override these default. - The following labels defined by Google Cloud Platform: - `cloud.googleapis.com/location` describing the location where the operation happened, - `servicecontrol.googleapis.com/user_agent` describing the user agent of the API request, - `servicecontrol.googleapis.com/service_agent` describing the service used to handle the API request (e.g. ESP), - `servicecontrol.googleapis.com/platform` describing the platform where the API is served, such as App Engine, Compute Engine, or Kubernetes Engine.

inputFields.name: logEntries
inputFields.description: Represents information to be logged.

inputFields.name: endTime
inputFields.description: End time of the operation. Required when the operation is used in ServiceController.Report, but optional when the operation is used in ServiceController.Check.

inputFields.name: resources
inputFields.description: The resources that are involved in the operation. The maximum supported number of entries in this field is 100.

inputFields.name: traceSpans
inputFields.description: Unimplemented. A list of Cloud Trace spans. The span names shall contain the id of the destination project which can be either the produce or the consumer project.

inputFields.name: operationName
inputFields.description: Fully qualified name of the operation. Reserved for future use.

inputFields.name: startTime
inputFields.description: Required. Start time of the operation.

inputFields.name: operationId
inputFields.description: Identity of the operation. This must be unique within the scope of the service that generated the operation. If the service calls Check() and Report() on the same operation, the two calls should carry the same id. UUID version 4 is recommended, though not required. In scenarios where an operation is computed from existing information and an idempotent id is desirable for deduplication purpose, UUID version 5 is recommended. See RFC 4122 for details.

inputFields.name: userLabels
inputFields.description: Private Preview. This feature is only available for approved services. User defined labels for the resource that this operation is associated with.

types.name: MetricValueSet_Input
types.description: Represents a set of metric values in the same metric. Each metric value in the set should have a unique combination of start time, end time, and label values.

inputFields.name: metricName
inputFields.description: The metric name defined in the service configuration.

inputFields.name: metricValues
inputFields.description: The values in this metric.

types.name: MetricValue_Input
types.description: Represents a single metric value.

inputFields.name: endTime
inputFields.description: The end of the time period over which this metric value's measurement applies. If not specified, google.api.servicecontrol.v1.Operation.end_time will be used.

inputFields.name: boolValue
inputFields.description: A boolean value.

inputFields.name: labels
inputFields.description: The labels describing the metric value. See comments on google.api.servicecontrol.v1.Operation.labels for the overriding relationship. Note that this map must not contain monitored resource labels.

inputFields.name: startTime
inputFields.description: The start of the time period over which this metric value's measurement applies. The time period has different semantics for different metric types (cumulative, delta, and gauge). See the metric definition documentation in the service configuration for details. If not specified, google.api.servicecontrol.v1.Operation.start_time will be used.

inputFields.name: doubleValue
inputFields.description: A double precision floating point value.

inputFields.name: stringValue
inputFields.description: A text string value.

inputFields.name: int64Value
inputFields.description: A signed 64-bit integer value.

types.name: Money_Input
types.description: Represents an amount of money with its currency type.

inputFields.name: currencyCode
inputFields.description: The three-letter currency code defined in ISO 4217.

inputFields.name: units

inputFields.name: nanos

inputFields.description: The i'th linear bucket covers the interval [offset + (i-1) * width, offset + i * width) where i ranges from 1 to num_finite_buckets, inclusive.

Examples are: Trace: type.googleapis.com/google.monitoring.v3.SpanContext Literal string: type.googleapis.com/google.protobuf.StringValue Labels dropped during aggregation: type.googleapis.com/google.monitoring.v3.DroppedLabels There may be only a single attachment of any given message type in a single exemplar, and this is enforced by the system.

types.name: QuotaProperties_Input
types.description: Represents the properties needed for quota operations.

types.name: mutationInput_servicecontrol_services_check_input_operation_quotaProperties_quotaMode
types.description: Quota mode for this operation.

types.name: LogEntry_Input
types.description: An individual log entry.

inputFields.name: protoPayload
inputFields.description: The log entry payload, represented as a protocol buffer that is expressed as a JSON object. The only accepted type currently is AuditLog.

inputFields.name: insertId
inputFields.description: A unique ID for the log entry used for deduplication. If omitted, the implementation will generate one based on operation_id.

inputFields.name: labels
inputFields.description: A set of user-defined (key, value) data that provides additional information about the log entry.

inputFields.name: trace
inputFields.description: Optional. Resource name of the trace associated with the log entry, if any. If this field contains a relative resource name, you can assume the name is relative to `//tracing.googleapis.com`. Example: `projects/my-projectid/traces/06796866738c859f2f19b7cfb3214824`

inputFields.name: textPayload
inputFields.description: The log entry payload, represented as a Unicode string (UTF-8).

inputFields.name: name
inputFields.description: Required. The log to which this log entry belongs. Examples: `"syslog"`, `"book_log"`.

inputFields.name: timestamp
inputFields.description: The time the event described by the log entry occurred. If omitted, defaults to operation start time.

inputFields.name: structPayload
inputFields.description: The log entry payload, represented as a structure that is expressed as a JSON object.

types.name: LogEntrySourceLocation_Input
types.description: Additional information about the source code location that produced the log entry.

inputFields.name: file
inputFields.description: Optional. Source file name. Depending on the runtime environment, this might be a simple name or a fully-qualified name.

inputFields.name: line
inputFields.description: Optional. Line within the source file. 1-based; 0 indicates no line number available.

inputFields.name: function
inputFields.description: Optional. Human-readable name of the function or method being invoked, with optional context such as the class or package name. This information may be used in contexts such as the logs viewer, where a file and line number are less meaningful. The format can vary by language. For example: `qual.if.ied.Class.method` (Java), `dir/package.func` (Go), `function` (Python).

types.name: HttpRequest_Input
types.description: A common proto for logging HTTP requests. Only contains semantics defined by the HTTP specification. Product-specific logging information MUST be defined in a separate message.

inputFields.name: serverIp
inputFields.description: The IP address (IPv4 or IPv6) of the origin server that the request was sent to.

inputFields.name: requestUrl
inputFields.description: The scheme (http, https), the host name, the path, and the query portion of the URL that was requested. Example: `"http://example.com/some/info?color=red"`.

inputFields.name: referer
inputFields.description: The referer URL of the request, as defined in [HTTP/1.1 Header Field Definitions](https://www.w3.org/Protocols/rfc2616/rfc2616-sec14.html).

inputFields.name: requestSize
inputFields.description: The size of the HTTP request message in bytes, including the request headers and the request body.

inputFields.name: userAgent
inputFields.description: The user agent sent by the client. Example: `"Mozilla/4.0 (compatible; MSIE 6.0; Windows 98; Q312461; .NET CLR 1.0.3705)"`.

inputFields.name: protocol
inputFields.description: Protocol used for the request. Examples: "HTTP/1.1", "HTTP/2", "websocket"

inputFields.name: latency
inputFields.description: The request processing latency on the server, from the time the request was received until the response was sent.

inputFields.name: cacheHit
inputFields.description: Whether or not an entity was served from cache (with or without validation).

inputFields.name: status
inputFields.description: The response code indicating the status of the response. Examples: 200, 404.

inputFields.name: responseSize
inputFields.description: The size of the HTTP response message sent back to the client, in bytes, including the response headers and the response body.

inputFields.name: cacheLookup
inputFields.description: Whether or not a cache lookup was attempted.

inputFields.name: cacheFillBytes
inputFields.description: The number of HTTP response bytes inserted into cache. Set only when a cache fill was attempted.

inputFields.name: requestMethod
inputFields.description: The request method. Examples: `"GET"`, `"HEAD"`, `"PUT"`, `"POST"`.

inputFields.name: remoteIp
inputFields.description: The IP address (IPv4 or IPv6) of the client that issued the HTTP request. Examples: `"192.168.1.1"`, `"FE80::0202:B3FF:FE1E:8329"`.

inputFields.name: cacheValidatedWithOriginServer
inputFields.description: Whether or not the response was validated with the origin server before being served from cache. This field is only meaningful if `cache_hit` is True.

types.name: mutationInput_servicecontrol_services_check_input_operation_logEntries_items_severity
types.description: The severity of the log entry. The default value is `LogSeverity.DEFAULT`.

types.name: LogEntryOperation_Input
types.description: Additional information about a potentially long-running operation with which a log entry is associated.

inputFields.name: id
inputFields.description: Optional. An arbitrary operation identifier. Log entries with the same identifier are assumed to be part of the same operation.

inputFields.name: last
inputFields.description: Optional. Set this to True if this is the last log entry in the operation.

inputFields.name: producer
inputFields.description: Optional. An arbitrary producer identifier. The combination of `id` and `producer` must be globally unique. Examples for `producer`: `"MyDivision.MyBigCompany.com"`, `"github.com/MyProject/MyApplication"`.

inputFields.name: first
inputFields.description: Optional. Set this to True if this is the first log entry in the operation.

types.name: ResourceInfo_Input
types.description: Describes a resource associated with this operation.

inputFields.name: resourceLocation
inputFields.description: The location of the resource. If not empty, the resource will be checked against location policy. The value must be a valid zone, region or multiregion. For example: "europe-west4" or "northamerica-northeast1-a"

inputFields.name: resourceName
inputFields.description: Name of the resource. This is used for auditing purposes.

inputFields.name: permission
inputFields.description: The resource permission required for this request.

inputFields.name: resourceContainer
inputFields.description: The identifier of the parent of this resource instance. Must be in one of the following formats: - `projects/` - `folders/` - `organizations/`

types.name: TraceSpan_Input
types.description: A span represents a single operation within a trace. Spans can be nested to form a trace tree. Often, a trace contains a root span that describes the end-to-end latency, and one or more subspans for its sub-operations. A trace can also contain multiple root spans, or none at all. Spans do not need to be contiguous—there may be gaps or overlaps between spans in a trace.

inputFields.name: endTime
inputFields.description: The end time of the span. On the client side, this is the time kept by the local machine where the span execution ends. On the server side, this is the time when the server application handler stops running.

inputFields.name: name
inputFields.description: The resource name of the span in the following format: projects/[PROJECT_ID]/traces/[TRACE_ID]/spans/SPAN_ID is a unique identifier for a trace within a project; it is a 32-character hexadecimal encoding of a 16-byte array. [SPAN_ID] is a unique identifier for a span within a trace; it is a 16-character hexadecimal encoding of an 8-byte array.

inputFields.name: sameProcessAsParentSpan
inputFields.description: (Optional) Set this parameter to indicate whether this span is in the same process as its parent. If you do not set this parameter, Stackdriver Trace is unable to take advantage of this helpful information.

inputFields.name: spanId
inputFields.description: The [SPAN_ID] portion of the span's resource name.

inputFields.name: parentSpanId
inputFields.description: The [SPAN_ID] of this span's parent span. If this is a root span, then this field must be empty.

inputFields.name: startTime
inputFields.description: The start time of the span. On the client side, this is the time kept by the local machine where the span execution starts. On the server side, this is the time when the server's application handler starts running.

inputFields.name: childSpanCount
inputFields.description: An optional number of child spans that were generated while this span was active. If set, allows implementation to detect missing child spans.

types.name: Attributes_Input
types.description: A set of attributes, each in the format `[KEY]:[VALUE]`.

inputFields.name: droppedAttributesCount
inputFields.description: The number of attributes that were discarded. Attributes can be discarded because their keys are too long or because there are too many attributes. If this value is 0 then all attributes are valid.

types.name: Status_Input
types.description: The `Status` type defines a logical error model that is suitable for different programming environments, including REST APIs and RPC APIs. It is used by [gRPC](https://github.com/grpc). Each `Status` message contains three pieces of data: error code, error message, and error details. You can find out more about this error model and how to work with it in the [API Design Guide](https://cloud.google.com/apis/design/errors).

inputFields.name: code
inputFields.description: The status code, which should be an enum value of google.rpc.Code.

inputFields.name: message
inputFields.description: A developer-facing error message, which should be in English. Any user-facing error message should be localized and sent in the google.rpc.Status.details field, or localized by the client.

inputFields.name: details
inputFields.description: A list of messages that carry the error details. There is a common set of message types for APIs to use.

types.name: TruncatableString_Input
types.description: Represents a string that might be shortened to a specified length.

inputFields.name: value
inputFields.description: The shortened string. For example, if the original string is 500 bytes long and the limit of the string is 128 bytes, then `value` contains the first 128 bytes of the 500-byte string. Truncation always happens on a UTF8 character boundary. If there are multi-byte characters in the string, then the length of the shortened string might be less than the size limit.

inputFields.name: truncatedByteCount
inputFields.description: The number of bytes removed from the original string. If this value is 0, then the string was not shortened.

types.name: mutationInput_servicecontrol_services_check_input_operation_traceSpans_items_spanKind
types.description: Distinguishes between spans generated in a particular context. For example, two spans with the same name may be distinguished using `CLIENT` (caller) and `SERVER` (callee) to identify an RPC call.

types.name: mutationInput_servicecontrol_services_check_input_operation_importance
types.description: DO NOT USE. This is an experimental field.

types.name: ReportResponse
types.description: Response message for the Report method.

fields.name: serviceRolloutId
fields.description: The current service rollout id used to process the request.

fields.name: reportErrors
fields.description: Partial failures, one for each `Operation` in the request that failed processing. There are three possible combinations of the RPC status: 1. The combination of a successful RPC status and an empty `report_errors` list indicates a complete success where all `Operations` in the request are processed successfully. 2. The combination of a successful RPC status and a non-empty `report_errors` list indicates a partial success where some `Operations` in the request succeeded. Each `Operation` that failed processing has a corresponding item in this list. 3. A failed RPC status indicates a general non-deterministic failure. When this happens, it's impossible to know which of the 'Operations' in the request succeeded or failed.

fields.name: serviceConfigId
fields.description: The actual config id used to process the request.

types.name: ReportError
types.description: Represents the processing error of one Operation in the request.

fields.name: operationId
fields.description: The Operation.operation_id value from the request.

types.name: ReportRequest_Input
types.description: Request message for the Report method.

inputFields.name: operations
inputFields.description: Operations to be reported. Typically the service should report one operation per request. Putting multiple operations into a single request is allowed, but should be used only when multiple operations are natually available at the time of the report. There is no limit on the number of operations in the same ReportRequest, however the ReportRequest size should be no larger than 1MB. See ReportResponse.report_errors for partial failure behavior.

inputFields.name: serviceConfigId
inputFields.description: Specifies which version of service config should be used to process the request. If unspecified or no matching version can be found, the latest one will be used.

types.name: AllocateQuotaResponse
types.description: Response message for the AllocateQuota method.

fields.name: quotaMetrics
fields.description: Quota metrics to indicate the result of allocation. Depending on the request, one or more of the following metrics will be included: 1. Per quota group or per quota metric incremental usage will be specified using the following delta metric : "serviceruntime.googleapis.com/api/consumer/quota_used_count" 2. The quota limit reached condition will be specified using the following boolean metric : "serviceruntime.googleapis.com/quota/exceeded"

fields.name: serviceConfigId
fields.description: ID of the actual config used to process the request.

fields.name: allocateErrors
fields.description: Indicates the decision of the allocate.

fields.name: operationId
fields.description: The same operation_id value used in the AllocateQuotaRequest. Used for logging and diagnostics purposes.

fields.name: unusedArguments
fields.description: A list of label keys that were unused by the server in processing the request. Thus, for similar requests repeated in a certain future time window, the caller can choose to ignore these labels in the requests to achieve better client-side cache hits and quota aggregation for rate quota. This field is not populated for allocation quota checks.

types.name: QuotaError
types.description: Represents error information for QuotaOperation.

fields.name: description
fields.description: Free-form text that provides details on the cause of the error.

fields.name: subject
fields.description: Subject to whom this error applies. See the specific enum for more details on this field. For example, "clientip:" or "project:".

types.name: mutation_servicecontrol_services_allocateQuota_allocateErrors_items_code
types.description: Error code.

types.name: AllocateQuotaRequest_Input
types.description: Request message for the AllocateQuota method.

inputFields.name: serviceConfigId
inputFields.description: Specifies which version of service configuration should be used to process the request. If unspecified or no matching version can be found, the latest one will be used.

types.name: QuotaOperation_Input
types.description: Represents information regarding a quota operation.

inputFields.name: quotaMetrics
inputFields.description: Represents information about this operation. Each MetricValueSet corresponds to a metric defined in the service configuration. The data type used in the MetricValueSet must agree with the data type specified in the metric definition. Within a single operation, it is not allowed to have more than one MetricValue instances that have the same metric names and identical label value combinations. If a request has such duplicated MetricValue instances, the entire request is rejected with an invalid argument error. This field is mutually exclusive with method_name.

inputFields.name: labels
inputFields.description: Labels describing the operation.

inputFields.name: methodName
inputFields.description: Fully qualified name of the API method for which this quota operation is requested. This name is used for matching quota rules or metric rules and billing status rules defined in service configuration. This field should not be set if any of the following is true: (1) the quota operation is performed on non-API resources. (2) quota_metrics is set because the caller is doing quota override. Example of an RPC method name: google.example.library.v1.LibraryService.CreateShelf

inputFields.name: consumerId
inputFields.description: Identity of the consumer for whom this quota operation is being performed. This can be in one of the following formats: project:, project_number:, api_key:.

inputFields.name: operationId
inputFields.description: Identity of the operation. For Allocation Quota, this is expected to be unique within the scope of the service that generated the operation, and guarantees idempotency in case of retries. In order to ensure best performance and latency in the Quota backends, operation_ids are optimally associated with time, so that related operations can be accessed fast in storage. For this reason, the recommended token for services that intend to operate at a high QPS is Unix time in nanos + UUID

types.name: mutationInput_servicecontrol_services_allocateQuota_input_allocateOperation_quotaMode
types.description: Quota mode for this operation.

types.name: String
types.description: The `String` scalar type represents textual data, represented as UTF-8 character sequences. The String type is most often used by GraphQL to represent free-form human-readable text.

types.name: Boolean
types.description: The `Boolean` scalar type represents `true` or `false`.

types.name: __Schema
types.description: A GraphQL Schema defines the capabilities of a GraphQL server. It exposes all available types and directives on the server, as well as the entry points for query, mutation, and subscription operations.

fields.name: types
fields.description: A list of all types supported by this server.

fields.name: queryType
fields.description: The type that query operations will be rooted at.

fields.name: mutationType
fields.description: If this server supports mutation, the type that mutation operations will be rooted at.

fields.name: subscriptionType
fields.description: If this server support subscription, the type that subscription operations will be rooted at.

fields.name: directives
fields.description: A list of all directives supported by this server.

types.name: __Type
types.description:
The fundamental unit of any GraphQL Schema is the type. There are many kinds of types in GraphQL as represented by the `__TypeKind` enum.

Depending on the kind of a type, certain fields describe information about that type. Scalar types provide no information beyond a name, description and optional `specifiedByURL`, while Enum types provide their values. Object and Interface types provide the fields they describe. Abstract types, Union and Interface, provide the Object types possible at runtime. List and NonNull types compose other types.

types.name: __TypeKind
types.description: An enum describing what kind of type a given `__Type` is.

enumValues.name: SCALAR
enumValues.description: Indicates this type is a scalar.

enumValues.name: OBJECT
enumValues.description: Indicates this type is an object. `fields` and `interfaces` are valid fields.

enumValues.name: INTERFACE
enumValues.description: Indicates this type is an interface. `fields`, `interfaces`, and `possibleTypes` are valid fields.

enumValues.name: UNION
enumValues.description: Indicates this type is a union. `possibleTypes` is a valid field.

enumValues.name: ENUM
enumValues.description: Indicates this type is an enum. `enumValues` is a valid field.

enumValues.name: INPUT_OBJECT
enumValues.description: Indicates this type is an input object. `inputFields` is a valid field.

enumValues.name: LIST
enumValues.description: Indicates this type is a list. `ofType` is a valid field.

enumValues.name: NON_NULL
enumValues.description: Indicates this type is a non-null. `ofType` is a valid field.

types.name: __Field
types.description: Object and Interface types are described by a list of Fields, each of which has a name, potentially a list of arguments, and a return type.

types.name: __InputValue
types.description: Arguments provided to Fields or Directives and the input fields of an InputObject are represented as Input Values which describe their type and optionally a default value.

fields.name: defaultValue
fields.description: A GraphQL-formatted string representing the default value for this input value.

types.name: __EnumValue
types.description: One possible value for a given Enum. Enum values are unique values, not a placeholder for a string or numeric value. However an Enum value is returned in a JSON response as a string.

types.name: __Directive
types.description:
A Directive provides a way to describe alternate runtime execution and type validation behavior in a GraphQL document.

In some cases, you need to provide options to alter GraphQL's execution behavior in ways field arguments will not suffice, such as conditionally including or skipping a field. Directives provide this by describing additional information to the executor.

types.name: __DirectiveLocation
types.description: A Directive can be adjacent to many parts of the GraphQL language, a __DirectiveLocation describes one such possible adjacencies.

enumValues.name: QUERY
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to a query operation.

enumValues.name: MUTATION
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to a mutation operation.

enumValues.name: SUBSCRIPTION
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to a subscription operation.

enumValues.name: FIELD
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to a field.

enumValues.name: FRAGMENT_DEFINITION
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to a fragment definition.

enumValues.name: FRAGMENT_SPREAD
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to a fragment spread.

enumValues.name: INLINE_FRAGMENT
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to an inline fragment.

enumValues.name: VARIABLE_DEFINITION
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to a variable definition.

enumValues.name: SCHEMA
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to a schema definition.

enumValues.name: SCALAR
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to a scalar definition.

enumValues.name: OBJECT
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to an object type definition.

enumValues.name: FIELD_DEFINITION
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to a field definition.

enumValues.name: ARGUMENT_DEFINITION
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to an argument definition.

enumValues.name: INTERFACE
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to an interface definition.

enumValues.name: UNION
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to a union definition.

enumValues.name: ENUM
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to an enum definition.

enumValues.name: ENUM_VALUE
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to an enum value definition.

enumValues.name: INPUT_OBJECT
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to an input object type definition.

enumValues.name: INPUT_FIELD_DEFINITION
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to an input object field definition.

directives.name: skip
directives.description: Directs the executor to skip this field or fragment when the `if` argument is true.

directives.name: include
directives.description: Directs the executor to include this field or fragment only when the `if` argument is true.

directives.name: deprecated
directives.description: Marks an element of a GraphQL schema as no longer supported.

args.name: reason
args.description: Explains why this element was deprecated, usually also including a suggestion for how to access supported similar data. Formatted using the Markdown syntax, as specified by [CommonMark](https://commonmark.org/).

directives.name: specifiedBy
directives.description: Exposes a URL that specifies the behavior of this scalar.

args.name: url
args.description: The URL that specifies the behavior of this scalar.

directives.name: oneOf
directives.description: Indicates exactly one field must be supplied and this field must not be `null`.
